message:
fix(search): emit project_id in Tantivy hit metadata for scope enforcement (br-2tnl.6.4)

Tantivy's build_hit() was not extracting project_id from indexed documents,
causing all V3 search results to have project_id=None. The scope enforcement
layer treated these as project_id=0, which never matched any viewer's project
list, resulting in all Tantivy results being denied as cross-project.

Fix: Extract project_id (u64→i64) from the Tantivy document in build_hit()
and include it in the SearchHit metadata map.

Also adds 8 integration tests proving V3 scope enforcement parity:
- Cross-project denial through Lexical engine
- Same-project auto-policy allows through Lexical engine
- ContactsOnly policy denies unlinked viewer
- Recipient override of BlockAll policy
- Redaction behavior on Allow vs Deny verdicts
- Operator mode produces no audit summary
- Audit summary counts match verdicts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-2tnl.3.5","title":"T3.5: Wire Tantivy lexical engine into search_service behind rollout flag","description":"## Task

- Preserve scope/redaction post-processing contract.

## Deliverable

 {"id":"br-2tnl.3.6","title":"T3.6: Add structured query assistance (field hints + typo-tolerant suggestions)","description":"## Task
Implement structured-query assistance for better user search ergonomics.

## Scope
- Parse optional fielded query hints (e.g., `from:`, `thread:`, `project:`, `before:`, `after:`) and map into Search V3 filters.
- Provide typo-tolerant fallback/suggestions for malformed filters.
- Add deterministic `did_you_mean` / `applied_filter_hints` metadata in responses.
- Keep backward compatibility with plain free-text queries.

## User Value
Users can express sender/project/date constraints naturally and recover quickly when query syntax is imperfect.

## Deliverable
Query-assistance layer integrated into lexical/hybrid planner with test coverage.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Structured query assistance supports sender/project/date/thread/importance fields with typo-tolerant suggestions and deterministic ordering.
2. MCP responses and TUI search expose actionable correction hints without changing semantics of already-valid queries.
3. Unit tests cover typo recovery, unknown-field handling, and suggestion stability across lexical/semantic/hybrid modes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:45:46.528329013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T21:00:32.433032296Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T20:59:44.638428298Z","close_reason":"Completed: query-assistance metadata wired through DB/tool/TUI surfaces with tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parser","search","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.3.6","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:11.700310506Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.3.6","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:45:46.528329013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.3.6","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:11.055493615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.3.6","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:11.366921048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":385,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.3.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: implemented structured query-assistance parser in search-core () with canonical hint extraction (), alias normalization, typo-tolerant deterministic did-you-mean suggestions (edit-distance bounded), quoted-value parsing, and plain-text compatibility (unknown field:value tokens preserved). -{"id":"br-2tnl.4","title":"[track] Semantic Embeddings and Vector Search Plane","description":"## Purpose
Add semantic retrieval infrastructure with embedding generation, vector storage, and filtered ANN/exact search.

## Scope
- Canonicalization pipeline for embedding text.
- Embedder abstraction and model registry (hash/fast/quality).
- Embedding persistence/versioning and vector index build/update.
- Background jobs for incremental embedding refresh.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Canonicalization, embedder registry, and embedding persistence are implemented.
- Vector retrieval supports metadata-aware search with deterministic top-k behavior.
- Background embedding/index refresh pipeline is operational.\n\nPlan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"track","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:36:26.328465082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:16:38.974712096Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["embeddings","search","semantic","vector"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:36:26.328465082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:35.042774772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:36.237934742Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":232,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track 4 reasoning:

- Semantic retrieval should be built as an additive plane with clear model/version boundaries, not hardwired to one embedding backend.
- Canonicalization + content hashing are essential to avoid unnecessary re-embedding churn and to support reproducible benchmarks.
- Non-goal: immediate reranking sophistication. This track delivers robust semantic candidate retrieval and lifecycle plumbing.
- Operational concern: embedding generation can be expensive; batching and retry/backpressure are mandatory.","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:39:13Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-2tnl.4.1","title":"T4.1: Implement embedding text canonicalization and hashing pipeline","description":"## Task
Implement canonicalization pipeline for embedding text inputs.

## Scope
- markdown normalization and noise stripping.
- deterministic hashing for dedupe/change detection.
- policy hooks for excluding sensitive fields.
- parity checks against patterns used in CASS/XF.

## Deliverable
Canonicalization module shared by embedding generation and tests.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Embedding canonicalization produces stable text payloads from indexed documents.
2. Hashing strategy detects semantic-input changes without false churn.
3. Unit tests validate canonicalization and hash stability across edge cases.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:02.915229433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:06:25.044536362Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:06:25.044449249Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canonicalization","search","semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:45.670713372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:02.915229433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":352,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation guidance (enrichment): The canonicalization pipeline should follow the CASS pattern from /dp/coding_agent_session_search but adapted for Agent Mail's document types:

1. Text normalization: (a) Strip GFM markdown to plain text (headers, links, code fences, emphasis), (b) Collapse consecutive whitespace to single space, (c) Normalize Unicode (NFC), (d) Lowercase for embedding (preserve original for display).

2. Sensitive field exclusion: (a) Strip inline token patterns (AGENT_MAIL_TOKEN=..., Bearer ...), (b) Apply the existing scrub module's redaction patterns (from mcp-agent-mail-share/src/scrub.rs), (c) Policy hook: accept a CanonPolicy enum { Full, RedactSecrets, FieldsOnly(Vec<String>) }.

3. Deterministic hashing: (a) Hash the canonicalized text with BLAKE2b-256 (already in deps via argon2 chain), (b) Store hash alongside embedding for change detection, (c) On message update: re-hash, compare, re-embed only if changed.

4. Document-type-specific extraction: (a) Messages: concat subject + body_md (with configurable weight), (b) Agents: concat name + program + model + task_description, (c) Projects: concat slug + human_key.

5. Tests: (a) roundtrip: canonical(text) is idempotent (canonical(canonical(x)) == canonical(x)), (b) markdown stripping: verified against 10+ GFM patterns (tables, code blocks, nested lists), (c) hash stability: same input always produces same hash across runs, (d) redaction: AGENT_MAIL_TOKEN=xxx is stripped before embedding, (e) Unicode: NFC normalization of decomposed chars.

Location: crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/canonical.rs (new file in the new search-core crate from br-2tnl.2.1).","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:42:01Z"},{"id":364,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented canonical.rs: 6 public functions (strip_markdown, redact_secrets, normalize_text, extract_text, canonicalize, canonicalize_and_hash, content_hash), 3 policy modes (Full/RedactSecrets/TitleOnly), 11 secret patterns (GitHub/Slack/OpenAI/AWS/Bearer/JWT/PEM/Anthropic/GitLab/env vars), comprehensive markdown stripping (14 GFM patterns). 43 tests passing.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:06:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-2tnl.4.2","title":"T4.2: Add embedder abstraction + model registry (hash/fast/quality)","description":"## Task

3. Tests verify model routing and registry integrity.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:03.143748343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T07:59:26.246193579Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T07:59:26.246173201Z","close_reason":"Implemented embedder abstraction and model registry with:

All tests pass, no clippy warnings in embedder.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["models","search","semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:03.143748343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:40.861730691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2tnl.4.3","title":"T4.3: Implement embedding persistence schema and invalidation semantics","description":"## Task

2. Invalidation semantics handle document mutation and model upgrades correctly.

Tests validate persistence correctness and invalidation triggers.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:03.376185468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:14:58.871804938Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:14:58.871782816Z","close_reason":"Implemented embedding persistence schema and invalidation semantics:

All code compiles and passes clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["db","search","semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4.3","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:03.376185468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4.3","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:12.387454011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -884,7 +884,7 @@
 {"id":"br-2tnl.5.10.8","title":"T5.10.8: Observability and metrics for two-tier search hardening","description":"## Objective

Comprehensive observability for two-tier search to enable debugging, monitoring, and performance tuning.

## Background: Alien Artifact Quality

Following /alien-artifact-coding principles, observability must be:
- **Self-documenting**: Metrics explain themselves
- **Deterministic**: Same inputs → same trace structure
- **Minimal overhead**: <1% latency impact when disabled
- **Complete**: No blind spots in the search pipeline

## Metrics to Implement

### 1. Initialization Metrics

```rust
pub struct TwoTierInitMetrics {
    /// Unix timestamp of initialization
    pub init_timestamp: i64,
    /// Duration of fast embedder load (ms)
    pub fast_embedder_load_ms: u64,
    /// Duration of quality embedder load (ms)
    pub quality_embedder_load_ms: u64,
    /// Availability status
    pub availability: TwoTierAvailability,
    /// Number of init attempts (should be 1)
    pub init_attempts: u32,
}
```

### 2. Search Metrics (per query)

```rust
pub struct TwoTierSearchMetrics {
    /// Query text hash (for correlation)
    pub query_hash: u64,
    /// Fast embedding latency (µs)
    pub fast_embed_us: u64,
    /// Fast search latency (µs)
    pub fast_search_us: u64,
    /// Quality embedding latency (µs, 0 if skipped)
    pub quality_embed_us: u64,
    /// Quality scoring latency (µs, 0 if skipped)
    pub quality_score_us: u64,
    /// Score blending latency (µs)
    pub blend_us: u64,
    /// Number of candidates from fast search
    pub fast_candidate_count: usize,
    /// Number of candidates refined
    pub refined_count: usize,
    /// Was quality refinement performed?
    pub was_refined: bool,
    /// Did refinement change ranking?
    pub ranking_changed: bool,
}
```

### 3. Index Metrics

```rust
pub struct TwoTierIndexMetrics {
    /// Total documents in index
    pub doc_count: usize,
    /// Documents with quality embeddings
    pub quality_doc_count: usize,
    /// Quality coverage ratio (0.0-1.0)
    pub quality_coverage: f32,
    /// Fast embedding memory usage (bytes)
    pub fast_memory_bytes: usize,
    /// Quality embedding memory usage (bytes)
    pub quality_memory_bytes: usize,
    /// Total memory usage (bytes)
    pub total_memory_bytes: usize,
}
```

### 4. Aggregated Metrics (for Prometheus/metrics endpoint)

```rust
pub struct TwoTierAggregatedMetrics {
    /// Total searches performed
    pub total_searches: u64,
    /// Searches with quality refinement
    pub refined_searches: u64,
    /// Searches where refinement changed ranking
    pub ranking_changed_count: u64,
    /// P50 fast latency (µs)
    pub fast_latency_p50_us: u64,
    /// P95 fast latency (µs)
    pub fast_latency_p95_us: u64,
    /// P50 total latency (µs)
    pub total_latency_p50_us: u64,
    /// P95 total latency (µs)
    pub total_latency_p95_us: u64,
}
```

## Tracing Spans

```rust
#[instrument(skip(self, query_vec), fields(
    query_len = query.len(),
    limit = k,
))]
pub fn search_fast(&self, query_vec: &[f32], k: usize) -> Vec<ScoredResult> {
    // ... with nested spans for each phase
}
```

Required spans:
- `two_tier.init` - Bridge initialization
- `two_tier.search` - Top-level search
- `two_tier.embed_fast` - Fast embedding
- `two_tier.search_fast` - Fast index search
- `two_tier.embed_quality` - Quality embedding
- `two_tier.score_quality` - Quality scoring
- `two_tier.blend` - Score blending
- `two_tier.rerank` - Final reranking

## Logging Guidelines

```rust
// Structured logging with context
tracing::info!(
    target: "search.two_tier",
    query_hash = %seahash::hash(query.as_bytes()),
    fast_latency_us = fast_latency_us,
    quality_latency_us = quality_latency_us,
    was_refined = was_refined,
    ranking_changed = ranking_changed,
    "two-tier search complete"
);

// Warn on performance regression
if fast_latency_us > 5000 {
    tracing::warn!(
        target: "search.two_tier",
        fast_latency_us = fast_latency_us,
        threshold_us = 5000,
        "fast search exceeded latency threshold"
    );
}
```

## Implementation Plan

1. Add `TwoTierMetrics` module to search-core
2. Instrument init path with timing
3. Instrument search path with per-phase timing
4. Add metrics aggregation with sliding window
5. Expose metrics via public API
6. Add tracing spans with appropriate levels

## Files to Modify

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/two_tier.rs` - Add instrumentation
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/auto_init.rs` - Init metrics
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/metrics.rs` - New metrics module
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs` - Expose metrics

## Dependencies

- Should be implemented alongside T5.10.1, T5.10.2, T5.10.3
- Not a blocker, can be done in parallel","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:

When observability metrics are emitted, include actionable guidance for users:

1. ON STARTUP (TwoTierInitMetrics):
```
[INFO] Two-tier search initialized:
  Fast embedder: potion-128M (loaded in 234ms)
  Quality embedder: MiniLM-L6-v2 (loaded in 1.2s)
  Mode: full (fast + quality refinement)

# If quality unavailable:
[WARN] Two-tier search initialized in FAST-ONLY mode:
  Fast embedder: potion-128M (loaded in 234ms)
  Quality embedder: UNAVAILABLE
    To enable quality refinement, install MiniLM-L6-v2:
    pip install fastembed && python -c "from fastembed import TextEmbedding; TextEmbedding('sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2')"
  Mode: fast-only (quality refinement disabled)
```

2. ON FIRST SEARCH (if degraded):
```
[INFO] Search complete in 2ms (fast-only, quality embedder unavailable)
  Tip: Install quality model for improved relevance ranking
```

3. IN TUI STATUS BAR:
```
Search: fast+quality | Docs: 1,234 (72% quality) | Avg latency: 145ms
# Or if degraded:
Search: FAST-ONLY ⚠️  | Docs: 1,234 | Avg latency: 2ms
```

4. IN HEALTH CHECK ENDPOINT:
```json
{
  "two_tier_search": {
    "status": "degraded",
    "fast_embedder": {"status": "ok", "model": "potion-128M"},
    "quality_embedder": {"status": "unavailable", "reason": "model not found", "install_hint": "pip install fastembed..."}
  }
}
```","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:29:32Z"},{"id":529,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ALERTING THRESHOLDS (add to metrics module):

## Automatic Alerts/Warnings

```rust
pub struct TwoTierAlertConfig {
    /// Warn if fast search exceeds this (µs)
    pub fast_latency_warn_us: u64,      // default: 5000 (5ms)
    /// Warn if quality refinement exceeds this (µs)
    pub quality_latency_warn_us: u64,    // default: 300000 (300ms)
    /// Warn if quality coverage drops below this
    pub quality_coverage_warn_pct: f32,  // default: 50.0
    /// Warn if index exceeds this many docs
    pub index_size_warn_docs: usize,     // default: 80000
    /// Warn if memory usage exceeds this (bytes)
    pub memory_warn_bytes: usize,        // default: 500MB
}

impl TwoTierMetrics {
    pub fn check_alerts(&self, config: &TwoTierAlertConfig) {
        if self.search.fast_search_us > config.fast_latency_warn_us {
            tracing::warn!(
                target: "search.two_tier.alert",
                fast_latency_us = self.search.fast_search_us,
                threshold_us = config.fast_latency_warn_us,
                "Fast search latency exceeded threshold"
            );
        }
        
        if self.index.quality_coverage < config.quality_coverage_warn_pct / 100.0 {
            tracing::warn!(
                target: "search.two_tier.alert",
                coverage_pct = self.index.quality_coverage * 100.0,
                threshold_pct = config.quality_coverage_warn_pct,
                "Quality embedding coverage below threshold"
            );
        }
        
        // ... similar for other thresholds
    }
}
```

## Alert Test Cases (add to T5.10.8 tests)

test_alert_on_slow_fast_search
test_alert_on_low_quality_coverage
test_alert_on_large_index
test_no_alert_when_within_thresholds","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:33:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-2tnl.5.10.9","title":"T5.10.9: SIMD and memory optimization for two-tier search","description":"## Objective

Profile-driven performance optimization for two-tier search following /extreme-software-optimization principles.

## Background: Current Implementation Analysis

The current SIMD dot product (two_tier.rs:548-597) uses `wide::f32x8`:

```rust
#[inline]
pub fn dot_product_f16_simd(embedding: &[f16], query: &[f32]) -> f32 {
    let chunks = embedding.len() / 8;
    let mut sum = f32x8::ZERO;

    for i in 0..chunks {
        let base = i * 8;
        let emb_f32 = [
            f32::from(embedding[base]),
            f32::from(embedding[base + 1]),
            // ... 6 more loads
        ];
        let q_arr: [f32; 8] = query[base..base + 8].try_into().expect("...");
        sum += f32x8::from(emb_f32) * f32x8::from(q_arr);
    }
    // ...
}
```

### Identified Bottlenecks

1. **f16→f32 conversion**: 8 sequential conversions per chunk
2. **Array construction**: Manual array building in hot loop
3. **Bounds checking**: `try_into().expect()` in hot path
4. **Memory layout**: f16 embeddings not aligned for SIMD

## Optimization Strategies

### 1. Batch f16→f32 Conversion

Use SIMD for f16 conversion (requires half crate with simd feature):

```rust
#[cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")]
#[inline]
unsafe fn f16x8_to_f32x8(ptr: *const f16) -> f32x8 {
    // Use _mm256_cvtph_ps intrinsic if available (F16C)
    // Or batch convert with lookup table
}
```

### 2. Prefetch for Cache Efficiency

```rust
#[inline]
pub fn dot_product_f16_simd_prefetch(embedding: &[f16], query: &[f32]) -> f32 {
    const PREFETCH_DISTANCE: usize = 4; // chunks ahead
    
    for i in 0..chunks {
        // Prefetch upcoming embeddings
        if i + PREFETCH_DISTANCE < chunks {
            std::arch::x86_64::_mm_prefetch(
                embedding.as_ptr().add((i + PREFETCH_DISTANCE) * 8) as *const i8,
                std::arch::x86_64::_MM_HINT_T0,
            );
        }
        // ... compute
    }
}
```

### 3. Memory Layout Optimization

Currently embeddings are stored row-major. Consider:
- **Interleaved layout**: Fast and quality embeddings interleaved for cache locality
- **Aligned storage**: Ensure 32-byte alignment for AVX

```rust
#[repr(C, align(32))]
struct AlignedEmbeddings {
    fast: Vec<f16>,
    quality: Vec<f16>,
}
```

### 4. Batch Query Processing

For multiple queries, batch process to amortize overhead:

```rust
pub fn search_batch(&self, queries: &[Vec<f32>], k: usize) -> Vec<Vec<ScoredResult>> {
    // Parallel query processing with work stealing
    queries.par_iter()
        .map(|q| self.search_fast(q, k))
        .collect()
}
```

### 5. Top-K Optimization

Current: BinaryHeap with Reverse wrapper
Alternative: Use selection algorithms for better cache behavior

```rust
// For small k, partial sort may be faster
if k < 32 {
    // Use nth_element-style partitioning
} else {
    // Use heap
}
```

## Profiling Requirements

Before optimization, profile with:

```bash
# CPU profiling
cargo flamegraph --package mcp-agent-mail-search-core --bench search_bench

# Memory profiling
valgrind --tool=cachegrind ./target/release/search_bench

# Instruction-level analysis
perf stat -e cycles,instructions,cache-misses ./target/release/search_bench
```

## Benchmarking Framework

```rust
#[bench]
fn bench_dot_product_f16_256dim(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let embedding: Vec<f16> = (0..256).map(|i| f16::from_f32(i as f32 * 0.01)).collect();
    let query: Vec<f32> = (0..256).map(|i| i as f32 * 0.01).collect();
    
    b.iter(|| {
        black_box(dot_product_f16_simd(black_box(&embedding), black_box(&query)))
    });
}

#[bench]
fn bench_search_fast_1000_docs(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let index = build_test_index(1000);
    let query = make_random_query(256);
    
    b.iter(|| {
        black_box(index.search_fast(black_box(&query), 10))
    });
}
```

## Performance Targets

Based on bake-off results:
- Fast embedding: <1ms for any query
- Fast search (1K docs): <1ms
- Fast search (10K docs): <5ms
- Fast search (100K docs): <50ms

## Implementation Plan

1. **Week 1**: Profiling and baseline benchmarks
2. **Week 2**: SIMD conversion optimization
3. **Week 3**: Memory layout optimization
4. **Week 4**: Top-K algorithm optimization

## Files to Modify

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/two_tier.rs` - Core optimizations
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/benches/` - Add benchmarks
- Consider new `simd.rs` module for architecture-specific code

## Dependencies

- Requires stable implementation first (T5.10.1, T5.10.2, T5.10.3)
- Not a blocker for functionality, only performance","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:

- Evaluate interleaved vs row-major layout
- Re-run benchmarks

Phase 4 - TOP-K ALGORITHM (diminishing returns expected):

- Profile to verify this is actually a hotspot before optimizing

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

Production-grade fusion function with reproducible ordering.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
1. RRF fusion combines candidate lists deterministically and exposes contribution details for explainability.
2. Fusion handles sparse and imbalanced candidate pools without pathological ranking behavior.
3. Unit tests validate formula correctness and deterministic ranking output.\n\n

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:04.377945187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:36:54.577008056Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hybrid","rrf","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.2","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:04.377945187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.2","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:41.694650332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":354,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation guidance (enrichment): RRF fusion is the heart of hybrid search. Reference: /dp/xf/src/hybrid.rs.

Core algorithm:
  rrf_score(doc) = sum over sources s: 1.0 / (k + rank_in_source_s(doc))
  where k is the RRF constant (default 60, configurable via AM_SEARCH_RRF_K env var).

Deterministic tie-breaking chain (CRITICAL for reproducibility):
  1. rrf_score descending (f64, with epsilon comparison for near-ties)
  2. lexical_score descending (break ties favoring lexical matches)
  3. doc_id ascending (absolute determinism)

Explain payload per hit:
  struct FusionExplain {
      lexical_rank: Option<usize>,    // rank in lexical pool (None if absent)
      lexical_score: Option<f64>,
      semantic_rank: Option<usize>,   // rank in semantic pool
      semantic_score: Option<f64>,
      rrf_score: f64,                 // final fused score
      source_contributions: Vec<(String, f64)>,  // [(source_name, 1/(k+rank))]
  }

Deduplication: docs appearing in both pools get scores from BOTH. Docs in only one pool get score from that pool only (the other contribution is 0, NOT penalized).

Pagination: apply AFTER fusion and dedup. Keep full fused result set in memory (typical size: 2*pool_size <= 200 docs), slice for requested page.

Tests:
  (a) Two pools of 10 docs each, 3 overlapping: verify fused list has 17 unique docs with correct scores.
  (b) Tie-breaking: two docs with identical rrf_score, different lexical_score: verify deterministic ordering.
  (c) Single-source doc: verify score = 1/(k + rank_in_that_source), not penalized for missing source.
  (d) Empty pool: verify other pool's results pass through unmodified.
  (e) Explain: verify per-hit explain includes both source contributions.
  (f) Pagination: verify offset/limit applied AFTER fusion.
  (g) Determinism: run same query 100 times, verify identical ordering.

Location: crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/fusion.rs","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:42:53Z"},{"id":400,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RRF fusion complete: fusion.rs with RrfConfig, fuse_rrf, FusionExplain, pagination. 11 tests passing. Ready for integration.","created_at":"2026-02-12T09:01:52Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-2tnl.5.3","title":"T5.3: Add two-tier fast→quality progressive refinement workflow","description":"## Task
Implement two-tier progressive search flow (fast then quality refinement).

## Scope
- fast-first response path for low latency.
- asynchronous quality refinement path.
- blending function and cutoff policy.
- cancellation and timeout behavior.

## Deliverable
Two-tier search API that can stream/refine results deterministically.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Two-tier pipeline executes fast pass then quality refinement with configurable triggers and budgets.
2. Progressive refinement improves quality while respecting latency budgets.
3. Integration tests validate stage transitions and timeout behavior.\n\nPlan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope

 {"id":"br-2tnl.5.4","title":"T5.4: Integrate cross-encoder reranker with configurable blend policy","description":"## Task
Integrate cross-encoder reranker for top-k candidate refinement.

## Scope
- reranker interface + model selection.
- configurable rerank cutoff and score blending.
- fallback behavior when reranker unavailable.
- auditability of rerank impact in explain payload.

## Deliverable
Optional rerank stage improving precision without destabilizing latency budgets.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Cross-encoder reranker integrates as optional refinement step with configurable blend policy.
2. Reranker failures degrade gracefully to fusion-only ranking.
3. Tests validate rerank impact, fallback behavior, and deterministic blending.\n\nPlan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:04.842708350Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:16:37.102063823Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["quality","reranking","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.4","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:46.720403195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.4","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:04.842708350Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.4","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:14.017927790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2tnl.5.5","title":"T5.5: Add hybrid cache/warmup/merged-explain infrastructure","description":"## Task
Implement search caching, warmup, and score explainability across hybrid tiers.

## Scope
- query-result cache keys including filters/mode.
- warm workers for lexical/semantic/rerank resources.
- cache invalidation on index epoch changes.
- explain object merging across lexical/semantic/rerank stages.

## Deliverable
Low-latency hybrid stack with transparent scoring metadata.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
1. Hybrid cache, warmup, and merged-explain infrastructure is integrated on top of T5.6, T5.7, and T5.8 outputs without duplicate keying logic.
2. Cache and explain payloads include lexical, semantic, rerank, and diversity-control contributions in a stable schema.
3. Integration tests verify cache hit/miss behavior, warmup behavior, and explain completeness under concurrent diversified ranking.\n\n
